query:
You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.49 (commit: c158350a).

## Issue to Resolve

`wrapping`: raw string false positive `Missing newline after "{"`
I have some code using `let` blocks inside raw strings, something like:
```
package test

class QueryGenerator {
    fun getQueryString(query: QueryRequest): String {
        val q = """
            SELECT *
            FROM table
            WHERE 1 = 1
            ${query.gameId?.let { "AND id = ?" } ?: ""}    
        """
        return q
    }
}

data class QueryRequest(
    val gameId: String?,
)
```

## Expected Behavior
This should cause no violations of the `wrapping` rule, regardless of the string size.

## Observed Behavior
In the code above, if I replace `table` by `table_name_long_enough_so_max_line_length_is_met_and_wrapping_rule_is_violated_and_generates_an_error`, for example (anything that makes the raw string size go above `max_line_length` seems to work), then I start seeing:
```
QueryGenerator.kt:9:34: Missing newline after "{" (wrapping)
```

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Start with the code above
2. Make the raw string big enough
3. Run ktlint 0.48+ in the file

## Your Environment
<!--- Include as many relevant details about the environment you experienced the bug in -->
* Version of ktlint used: 0.48.1
* Relevant parts of the `.editorconfig` settings: `max_line_length = 160`
* Name and version (or code for custom task) of integration used (Gradle plugin, Maven plugin, command line, custom Gradle task): ktlint CLI
* Operating System and version: Arch Linux (rolling as of 2023-01-15)

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

This issue is very tricky to reproduce, actually, this might not be related to `max_line_length` at all. I just tried moving the entire content of the raw string to a single line and the error is gone, even with a very long raw string.
The exact values I provided causes the issue 100% of the times I tried, though, so I hope that's enough to reproduce the issue on your side.
Can be reproduced. The error is introduced in `0.48.x`.
Thanks for the quick reply :)
This is my only blocker to update to 0.48, so let me know if I can help with anything.
You can try to solve this issue via a PR ;-)

Otherwise. just disable the rule (https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/faq/#how-do-i-suppress-errors-for-a-lineblockfile) and start using the new version.

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/WrappingRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

113â†’            BLOCK -> beforeVisitBlock(node, autoCorrect, emit)
114â†’            LPAR, LBRACKET -> rearrangeBlock(node, autoCorrect, emit) // TODO: LT
115â†’            SUPER_TYPE_LIST -> rearrangeSuperTypeList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
116â†’            VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST, VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST -> rearrangeValueList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
117â†’            ARROW -> rearrangeArrow(node, autoCorrect, emit)
118â†’            WHITE_SPACE -> line += node.text.count { it == '\n' }

154â†’                }

352â†’                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(nextSibling, autoCorrect, emit)
353â†’                }
354â†’            }
355â†’        }
356â†’    }
357â†’
358â†’    private fun ASTNode.isLastValueParameter() =
359â†’        elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER &&
360â†’            siblings().none { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
361â†’
362â†’    private fun rearrangeClosingQuote(
363â†’        node: ASTNode,
364â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
365â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
366â†’    ) {
367â†’        node
368â†’            .treeParent
369â†’            .takeIf { it.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE }
370â†’            ?.let { it.psi as KtStringTemplateExpression }
371â†’            ?.takeIf { it.isMultiLine() }
372â†’            ?.takeIf { it.isFollowedByTrimIndent() || it.isFollowedByTrimMargin() }
373â†’            ?.takeIf { node.treePrev.text.isNotBlank() }
374â†’            ?.let {
375â†’                // rewriting
376â†’                // """
377â†’                //     text
378â†’                // _""".trimIndent()
379â†’                // to
380â†’                // """
381â†’                //     text
382â†’                // _
383â†’                // """.trimIndent()
384â†’                emit(
385â†’                    node.startOffset,
386â†’                    "Missing newline before \"\"\"",
387â†’                    true,
388â†’                )
389â†’                if (autoCorrect) {
390â†’                    node as LeafPsiElement
391â†’                    node.rawInsertBeforeMe(LeafPsiElement(LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY, "\n"))
392â†’                }
393â†’                LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before (closing) \"\"\"" }
394â†’            }
395â†’    }
396â†’
397â†’    private fun mustBeFollowedByNewline(node: ASTNode): Boolean {
398â†’        // find EOL token (last token before \n)
399â†’        // if token is in lTokenSet
400â†’        //     find matching rToken
401â†’        //     return true if there is no newline after the rToken
402â†’        // return false
403â†’        val nextCodeSibling = node.nextCodeSibling() // e.g. BINARY_EXPRESSION
404â†’        var lToken = nextCodeSibling?.nextLeaf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }?.prevCodeLeaf()
405â†’        if (lToken != null && lToken.elementType !in LTOKEN_SET) {
406â†’            // special cases:
407â†’            // x = y.f({ z ->
408â†’            // })
409â†’            // x = y.f(0, 1,
410â†’            // 2, 3)
411â†’            lToken = lToken.prevLeaf { it.elementType in LTOKEN_SET || it == node }
412â†’        }
413â†’        if (lToken != null && lToken.elementType in LTOKEN_SET) {
414â†’            val rElementType = MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP[lToken.elementType]
415â†’            val rToken = lToken.nextSibling { it.elementType == rElementType }
416â†’            return rToken?.treeParent == lToken.treeParent
417â†’        }
418â†’        if (nextCodeSibling?.textContains('\n') == false) {
419â†’            return true
420â†’        }
421â†’        return false
422â†’    }
423â†’
424â†’    private fun rearrangeArrow(
425â†’        node: ASTNode,
426â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
427â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
428â†’    ) {
429â†’        val p = node.treeParent
430â†’        if (
431â†’            // check
432â†’            // `{ p -> ... }`
433â†’            // and
434â†’            // `when { m -> ... }`
435â†’            // only
436â†’            p.elementType.let { it != FUNCTION_LITERAL && it != WHEN_ENTRY } ||
437â†’            // ... and only if expression after -> spans multiple lines
438â†’            !p.textContains('\n') ||
439â†’            // permit
440â†’            // when {
441â†’            //     m -> 0 + d({
442â†’            //     })
443â†’            // }
444â†’            (p.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY && mustBeFollowedByNewline(node)) ||
445â†’            // permit
446â†’            // when (this) {
447â†’            //     in 0x1F600..0x1F64F, // Emoticons
448â†’            //     0x200D // Zero-width Joiner
449â†’            //     -> true
450â†’            // }
451â†’            (p.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY && node.prevLeaf()?.textContains('\n') == true)
452â†’        ) {
453â†’            return
454â†’        }
455â†’        if (!node.nextCodeLeaf()?.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
456â†’            requireNewlineAfterLeaf(node, autoCorrect, emit)
457â†’        }
458â†’        val r = node.nextSibling { it.elementType == RBRACE } ?: return
459â†’        if (!r.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
460â†’            requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(r, autoCorrect, emit, node.lineIndent())
461â†’        }
462â†’    }
463â†’
464â†’    private fun requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
465â†’        node: ASTNode,
466â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
467â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
468â†’        indent: String,
469â†’    ) {
470â†’        emit(
471â†’            node.startOffset - 1,
472â†’            """Missing newline before "${node.text}"""",
473â†’            true,
474â†’        )
475â†’        LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + ((if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before ${node.text}") }
476â†’        if (autoCorrect) {
477â†’            node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe("\n" + indent)
478â†’        }
479â†’    }
480â†’
481â†’    private fun requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
482â†’        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired: ASTNode,
483â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
484â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
485â†’        indent: String? = null,
486â†’        nodeToFix: ASTNode = nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired,
487â†’    ) {
488â†’        emit(
489â†’            nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.startOffset + 1,
490â†’            """Missing newline after "${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"""",
491â†’            true,
492â†’        )
493â†’        LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline after ${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}" }
494â†’        if (autoCorrect) {
495â†’            val tempIndent = indent ?: (nodeToFix.lineIndent() + indentConfig.indent)
496â†’            nodeToFix.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe("\n" + tempIndent)
497â†’        }
498â†’    }
499â†’
500â†’    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isMultiLine(): Boolean {
501â†’        for (child in node.children()) {
502â†’            if (child.elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY) {
503â†’                val v = child.text
504â†’                if (v == "\n") {
505â†’                    return true
506â†’                }
507â†’            }
508â†’        }
509â†’        return false
510â†’    }
511â†’
512â†’    private fun ASTNode.hasLineBreak(vararg ignoreElementTypes: IElementType): Boolean {
513â†’        if (isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) return true
514â†’        return if (ignoreElementTypes.isEmpty()) {
515â†’            textContains('\n')
516â†’        } else {
517â†’            elementType !in ignoreElementTypes &&
518â†’                children().any { c -> c.textContains('\n') && c.elementType !in ignoreElementTypes }
519â†’        }
520â†’    }
521â†’
522â†’    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedByTrimIndent() = isFollowedBy("trimIndent()")
523â†’
524â†’    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedByTrimMargin() = isFollowedBy("trimMargin()")
525â†’
526â†’    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedBy(callExpressionName: String) =
527â†’        this.node.nextSibling { it.elementType != DOT }
528â†’            .let { it?.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION && it.text == callExpressionName }
529â†’
530â†’    /**
531â†’     *  Allow for-statement in which only the expression contains a newline:
532â†’     *     for (foo in listOf(
533â†’     *         "foo-1",
534â†’     *         "foo-2"
535â†’     *     )) { ... }
536â†’     * but reject:
537â†’     *     for (
538â†’     *         foo in listOf(
539â†’     *             "foo-1",
540â†’     *             "foo-2"
541â†’     *         )
542â†’     *     ) { ... }
543â†’     */
544â†’    private fun ASTNode.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression(): Boolean {
545â†’        if (treeParent.elementType != ElementType.FOR) {
546â†’            return false
547â†’        }
548â†’        if (this.elementType != LPAR) {
549â†’            return treeParent.findChildByType(LPAR)!!.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression()
550â†’        }
551â†’        require(elementType == LPAR) {
552â†’            "Node should be the LPAR of the FOR loop"
553â†’        }
554â†’
555â†’        var node: ASTNode? = this
556â†’        while (node != null && node.elementType != RPAR) {
557â†’            if (node.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
558â†’                return false
559â†’            }
560â†’            node = node.nextSibling { true }
561â†’        }
562â†’        return true
563â†’    }
564â†’
565â†’    override fun afterVisitChildNodes(
566â†’        node: ASTNode,
567â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
568â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
569â†’    ) {
570â†’        if (node.elementType == BLOCK) {
571â†’            val startOfBlock = node.prevLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
572â†’            if (startOfBlock?.elementType != LBRACE) {
573â†’                return
574â†’            }
575â†’            val blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline = startOfBlock.nextLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
576â†’            val endOfBlock = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf().nextLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
577â†’            val blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline = endOfBlock?.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
578â†’            val wrapBlock =
579â†’                !blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline && (
580â†’                    blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline || node.textContains('\n')
581â†’                    )
582â†’            if (wrapBlock && endOfBlock != null) {
583â†’                requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
584â†’                    endOfBlock,
585â†’                    autoCorrect,
586â†’                    emit,
587â†’                    node.treeParent.lineIndent(),
588â†’                )
589â†’            }
590â†’        }
591â†’    }
592â†’
593â†’    private companion object {
594â†’        private val LTOKEN_SET = TokenSet.create(LPAR, LBRACE, LBRACKET, LT)
595â†’        private val RTOKEN_SET = TokenSet.create(RPAR, RBRACE, RBRACKET, GT)
596â†’        private val MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP =
597â†’            LTOKEN_SET.types.zip(
598â†’                RTOKEN_SET.types,
599â†’            ).toMap()
600â†’    }
601â†’}
602â†’

conversation_history.content:
1â†’# Changelog
  2â†’All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3â†’This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4â†’
  5â†’## Unreleased
  6â†’
  7â†’### Added
  8â†’
  9â†’### Removed
 10â†’
 11â†’### Fixed
 12â†’* Do not enable the experimental rules by default when `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` or `ktlint_disabled_rules` are set. ([#1771](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1771))
 13â†’* A function signature not having any parameters which exceeds the `max-line-length` should be ignored by rule `function-signature` ([#1773](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1773))
 14â†’
 15â†’### Changed
 16â†’
 17â†’## [0.48.1] - 2023-01-03
 18â†’
 19â†’### Added
 20â†’
 21â†’### Removed
 22â†’
 23â†’### Fixed
 24â†’
 25â†’* An enumeration class having a primary constructor and in which the list of enum entries is followed by a semicolon then do not remove the semicolon in case it is followed by code element `no-semi` ([#1733](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1733))
 26â†’* Add API so that KtLint API consumer is able to process a Kotlin script snippet without having to specify a file path ([#1738](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1738))
 27â†’* Disable the `standard:filename` rule whenever Ktlint CLI is run with option `--stdin` ([#1742](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1742))
 28â†’* Fix initialization of the logger when `--log-level` is specified. Throw exception when an invalid value is passed. ([#1749](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1749))
 29â†’* Fix loading of custom rule set JARs.
 30â†’* Rules provided via a custom rule set JAR (Ktlint CLI) or by an API provider are enabled by default. Only rules in the `experimental` rule set are disabled by default. ([#1747](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1747))
 31â†’
 32â†’### Changed
 33â†’
 34â†’* Update Kotlin development version to `1.8.0` and Kotlin version to `1.8.0`.
 35â†’
 36â†’## [0.48.0] - 2022-12-15
 37â†’
 38â†’### Indent rule
 39â†’
 40â†’The `indent` rule has been rewritten from scratch. Solving problems in the old algorithm was very difficult. With the new algorithm this becomes a lot easier. Although the new implementation of the rule has been compared against several open source projects containing over 400,000 lines of code, it is still likely that new issues will be discovered. Please report your indentation issues so that these can be fixed as well.
 41â†’
 42â†’### `.editorconfig` property to disable rules
 43â†’
 44â†’In the previous release (0.47.x), the `.editorconfig`  property `disabled_rules` was deprecated and replaced with `ktlint_disabled_rules`. This latter property has now been deprecated as well in favour of a more flexible and better maintainable solution. Rule and rule sets can now be enabled/disabled with a separate property per rule (set). Please read [deprecation of (ktlint_)disable_rules property](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/faq/#why-is-editorconfig-property-disabled_rules-deprecated-and-how-do-i-resolve-this) for more information.
 45â†’
 46â†’The KtLint CLI has not been changed. Although you can still use parameter `--experimental` to enable KtLint's Experimental rule set, you might want to set `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental = enabled` instead.
 47â†’
 48â†’### API Changes & RuleSet providers
 49â†’
 50â†’If you are not an API consumer or Rule Set provider then you can skip this section.
 51â†’
 52â†’#### Class relocations
 53â†’
 54â†’Classes below have been relocated:
 55â†’
 56â†’* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.UsesEditorConfigProperties.EditorConfigProperty` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfigProperty`. 
 57â†’* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.KtLintParseException` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.KtLintParseException`.
 58â†’* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleExecutionException` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.KtLintRuleException`.
 59â†’* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.format.internal.Color` has been moved to `com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.format.Color`.
 60â†’* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.plain.internal.Color` has been moved to `com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.plain.Color`.
 61â†’
 62â†’#### Invoking `lint` and `format`
 63â†’
 64â†’This is the last release that supports the `ExperimentalParams` to invoke the `lint` and `format` functions of KtLint. The `ExperimentalParams` contains a mix of configuration settings which are not dependent on the file/code which is to be processed. Other parameters in that class describe the code/file to be processed but can be configured inconsistently (for example a file with name "foo.kt" could be marked as a Kotlin Script file).
 65â†’
 66â†’The static object `KtLint` is deprecated and replaced by class `KtLintRuleEngine` which is configured with `KtLintRuleEngineConfiguration`. The instance of the `KtLintRuleEngine` is intended to be reused for scanning all files in a project and should not be recreated per file.
 67â†’
 68â†’Both `lint` and `format` are simplified and can now be called for a code block or for an entire file.
 69â†’
 70â†’```kotlin
 71â†’import java.io.File
 72â†’
 73â†’// Define a reusable instance of the KtLint Rule Engine
 74â†’val ktLintRuleEngine = KtLintRuleEngine(
 75â†’  // Define configuration
 76â†’)
 77â†’
 78â†’
 79â†’// Process a collection of files
 80â†’val files: Set<File> // Collect files in a convenient way
 81â†’files.forEach(file in files) {
 82â†’    ktLintRuleEngine.lint(file) {
 83â†’        // Handle lint violations
 84â†’    }
 85â†’}
 86â†’
 87â†’// or process a code sample for a given filepath
 88â†’ktLintRuleEngine.lint(
 89â†’  code = "code to be linted",
 90â†’  filePath = Path("/path/to/source/file")
 91â†’) {
 92â†’  // Handle lint violations
 93â†’}
 94â†’```
 95â†’
 96â†’#### Retrieve `.editorconfig`s
 97â†’
 98â†’The list of `.editorconfig` files which will be accessed by KtLint when linting or formatting a given path can now be retrieved with the new API `KtLint.editorConfigFilePaths(path: Path): List<Path>`. 
 99â†’
100â†’This API can be called with either a file or a directory. It's intended usage is that it is called once with the root directory of a project before actually linting or formatting files of that project. When called with a directory path, all `.editorconfig` files in the directory or any of its subdirectories (except hidden directories) are returned. In case the given directory does not contain an `.editorconfig` file or if it does not contain the `root=true` setting, the parent directories are scanned as well until a root `.editorconfig` file is found.
101â†’
102â†’Calling this API with a file path results in the `.editorconfig` files that will be accessed when processing that specific file. In case the directory in which the file resides does not contain an `.editorconfig` file or if it does not contain the `root=true` setting, the parent directories are scanned until a root `.editorconfig` file is found.
103â†’
104â†’#### Psi filename replaces FILE_PATH_USER_DATA_KEY
105â†’
106â†’Constant `KtLint.FILE_PATH_USER_DATA_KEY` is deprecated and will be removed in KtLint version 0.49.0. The file name will be passed correctly to the node with element type FILE and can be retrieved as follows:
107â†’```kotlin
108â†’if (node.isRoot()) {
109â†’    val fileName = (node.psi as? KtFile)?.name
110â†’    ...
111â†’}
112â†’```
113â†’
114â†’### Added
115â†’* Wrap blocks in case the max line length is exceeded or in case the block contains a new line `wrapping` ([#1643](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issue/1643))
116â†’* patterns can be read in from `stdin` with the `--patterns-from-stdin` command line options/flags ([#1606](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1606))
117â†’* Add basic formatting for context receiver in `indent` rule and new experimental rule `context-receiver-wrapping` ([#1672](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issue/1672))
118â†’* Add naming rules for classes and objects (`class-naming`), functions (`function-naming`) and properties (`property-naming`) ([#44](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issue/44))
119â†’* Add new built-in reporter `plain-summary` which prints a summary the number of violation which have been autocorrected or could not be autocorrected, both split by rule. 
120â†’
121â†’
122â†’### Fixed
123â†’
124â†’* Let a rule process all nodes even in case the rule is suppressed for a node so that the rule can update the internal state ([#1644](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issue/1644))
125â†’* Read `.editorconfig` when running CLI with options `--stdin` and `--editorconfig` ([#1651](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issue/1651))
126â†’* Do not add a trailing comma in case a multiline function call argument is found but no newline between the arguments `trailing-comma-on-call-site` ([#1642](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issue/1642))
127â†’* Add missing `ktlint_disabled_rules` to exposed `editorConfigProperties` ([#1671](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issue/1671))
128â†’* Do not add a second trailing comma, if the original trailing comma is followed by a KDOC `trailing-comma-on-declaration-site` and `trailing-comma-on-call-site` ([#1676](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issue/1676))
129â†’* A function signature preceded by an annotation array should be handled similar as function preceded by a singular annotation `function-signature` ([#1690](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issue/1690))
130â†’* Fix offset of annotation violations
131â†’* Fix line offset when blank line found between class and primary constructor
132â†’* Remove needless blank line between class followed by EOL, and primary constructor
133â†’* Fix offset of unexpected linebreak before assignment
134â†’* Remove whitespace before redundant semicolon if the semicolon is followed by whitespace 
135â†’
136â†’### Changed
137â†’* Update Kotlin development version to `1.8.0-RC` and Kotlin version to `1.7.21`.
138â†’* The default value for trailing comma's on call site is changed to `true` unless the `android codestyle` is enabled. Note that KtLint from a consistency viewpoint *enforces* the trailing comma on call site while default IntelliJ IDEA formatting only *allows* the trailing comma but leaves it up to the developer's discretion. ([#1670](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1670))
139â†’* The default value for trailing comma's on declaration site is changed to `true` unless the `android codestyle` is enabled. Note that KtLint from a consistency viewpoint *enforces* the trailing comma on declaration site while default IntelliJ IDEA formatting only *allows* the trailing comma but leaves it up to the developer's discretion. ([#1669](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1669))
140â†’* CLI options `--debug`, `--trace`, `--verbose` and `-v` are replaced with `--log-level=<level>` or the short version `-l=<level>, see [CLI log-level](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/install/cli/#logging). ([#1632](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issue/1632))
141â†’* In CLI, disable logging entirely by setting `--log-level=none` or `-l=none` ([#1652](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issue/1652))
142â†’* Rewrite `indent` rule. Solving problems in the old algorithm was very difficult. With the new algorithm this becomes a lot easier. Although the new implementation of the rule has been compared against several open source projects containing over 400,000 lines of code, it is still likely that new issues will be discovered. Please report your indentation issues so that these can be fixed as well. ([#1682](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1682), [#1321](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1321), [#1200](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1200), [#1562](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1562), [#1563](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1563), [#1639](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1639))
143â†’* Add methods "ASTNode.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe" and "ASTNode.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe" as replacements for "LeafElement.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe" and "LeafElement.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe". The new methods are more versatile and allow code to be written more readable in most places. ([#1687](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1687))
144â†’* Rewrite `indent` rule. Solving problems in the old algorithm was very difficult. With the new algorithm this becomes a lot easier. Although the new implementation of the rule has been compared against several open source projects containing over 400,000 lines of code, it is still likely that new issues will be discovered. Please report your indentation issues so that these can be fixed as well. ([#1682](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1682), [#1321](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1321), [#1200](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1200), [#1562](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1562), [#1563](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1563), [#1639](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1639), [#1688](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1688))
145â†’* Add support for running tests on `java 19`, remove support for running tests on `java 18`.
146â†’* Update `io.github.detekt.sarif4k:sarif4k` version to `0.2.0` ([#1701](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1701)).
147â†’
148â†’## [0.47.1] - 2022-09-07
149â†’
150â†’### Fixed
151â†’* Do not add trailing comma in empty parameter/argument list with comments (`trailing-comma-on-call-site`, `trailing-comma-on-declaration-site`) ([#1602](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issue/1602))
152â†’* Fix class cast exception when specifying a non-string editorconfig setting in the default ".editorconfig" ([#1627](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issue/1627))
153â†’* Fix indentation before semi-colon when it is pushed down after inserting a trailing comma  ([#1609](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issue/1609))
154â†’* Do not show deprecation warning about property "disabled_rules" when using CLi-parameter `--disabled-rules` ([#1599](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1599))
155â†’* Traversing directory hierarchy at Windows ([#1600](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1600))
156â†’* Ant-style path pattern support ([#1601](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1601))
157â†’* Apply `@file:Suppress` on all toplevel declarations ([#1623](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1623)) 
158â†’
159â†’### Changed
160â†’* Display warning instead of error when no files are matched, and return with exit code 0. ([#1624](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1624))
161â†’
162â†’## [0.47.0] - 2022-08-19
163â†’
164â†’### API Changes & RuleSet providers
165â†’
166â†’If you are not an API consumer nor a RuleSet provider, then you can safely skip this section. Otherwise, please read below carefully and upgrade your usage of ktlint. In this and coming releases, we are changing and adapting important parts of our API in order to increase maintainability and flexibility for future changes. Please avoid skipping a releases as that will make it harder to migrate.
167â†’
168â†’#### Rule lifecycle hooks / deprecate RunOnRootOnly visitor modifier
169â†’
170â†’Up until ktlint 0.46 the Rule class provided only one life cycle hook. This "visit" hook was called in a depth-first-approach on all nodes in the file. A rule like the IndentationRule used the RunOnRootOnly visitor modifier to call this lifecycle hook for the root node only in combination with an alternative way of traversing the ASTNodes. Downside of this approach was that suppression of the rule on blocks inside a file was not possible ([#631](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/631)). More generically, this applied to all rules, applying alternative traversals of the AST.
171â†’
172â†’The Rule class now offers new life cycle hooks:
173â†’* beforeFirstNode: This method is called once before the first node is visited. It can be used to initialize the state of the rule before processing of nodes starts. The ".editorconfig" properties (including overrides) are provided as parameter.
174â†’* beforeVisitChildNodes: This method is called on a node in AST before visiting its child nodes. This is repeated recursively for the child nodes resulting in a depth first traversal of the AST. This method is the equivalent of the "visit" life cycle hooks. However, note that in KtLint 0.48, the UserData of the rootnode no longer provides access to the ".editorconfig" properties. This method can be used to emit Lint Violations and to autocorrect if applicable.
175â†’* afterVisitChildNodes: This method is called on a node in AST after all its child nodes have been visited. This method can be used to emit Lint Violations and to autocorrect if applicable.
176â†’* afterLastNode: This method is called once after the last node in the AST is visited. It can be used for teardown of the state of the rule.
177â†’
178â†’Optionally, a rule can stop the traversal of the remainder of the AST whenever the goal of the rule has been achieved. See KDoc on Rule class for more information.
179â†’
180â†’The "visit" life cycle hook will be removed in Ktlint 0.48. In KtLint 0.47 the "visit" life cycle hook will be called *only* when hook "beforeVisitChildNodes" is not overridden. It is recommended to migrate to the new lifecycle hooks in KtLint 0.47. Please create an issue, in case you need additional assistence to implement the new life cycle hooks in your rules.
181â†’
182â†’
183â†’#### Ruleset providing by Custom Rule Set Provider
184â†’
185â†’The KtLint engine needs a more fine-grained control on the instantiation of new Rule instances. Currently, a new instance of a rule can be created only once per file. However, when formatting files the same rule instance is reused for a second processing iteration in case a Lint violation has been autocorrected. By re-using the same rule instance, state of that rule might leak from the first to the second processing iteration.
186â†’
187â†’Providers of custom rule sets have to migrate the custom rule set JAR file. The current RuleSetProvider interface which is implemented in the custom rule set is deprecated and marked for removal in KtLint 0.48. Custom rule sets using the old RuleSetProvider interface will not be run in KtLint 0.48 or above.
188â†’
189â†’For now, it is advised to implement the new RuleSetProviderV2 interface without removing the old RuleSetProvider interface. In this way, KtLint 0.47 and above use the RuleSetProviderV2 interface and ignore the old RuleSetProvider interface completely. KtLint 0.46 and below only use the old RuleSetProvider interface.
190â†’
191â†’Adding the new interface is straight forward, as can be seen below:
192â†’
193â†’```
194â†’// Current implementation
195â†’public class CustomRuleSetProvider : RuleSetProvider {
196â†’    override fun get(): RuleSet = RuleSet(
197â†’        "custom",
198â†’        CustomRule1(),
199â†’        CustomRule2(),
200â†’    )

content.thinking:
Now let me understand the issue better. The issue is about a false positive "Missing newline after `{`" when using `let` blocks inside raw strings. The problem seems to be related to the `beforeVisitBlock` function in `WrappingRule.kt`.

Looking at the `beforeVisitBlock` function:
1. It finds the LBRACE (start of block)
2. It checks if the block is preceded by whitespace containing a newline
3. It checks if the block contains a newline or is followed by a newline
4. If `maxLineLength > 0`, it calculates whether the line length exceeds max line length

The issue is that when we have a raw string literal containing a `let` block, the block detection doesn't properly account for the fact that the block is inside a string template within a raw string.

Let me search for how this is handled and what element types are involved with string templates.

content.thinking:
The `isPartOfString` function checks if a node is inside a STRING_TEMPLATE. So if a `BLOCK` is inside a raw string template, `isPartOfString()` would return true.

Now let me understand the issue better. Looking at the problem code:
```kotlin
val q = """
    SELECT *
    FROM table_name_long_enough_so_max_line_length_is_met_and_wrapping_rule_is_violated_and_generates_an_error
    WHERE 1 = 1
    ${query.gameId?.let { "AND id = ?" } ?: ""}    
"""
```

Inside the raw string, there's a string template `${...}` that contains a lambda with `let { "AND id = ?" }`. The lambda contains a `BLOCK`.

The `beforeVisitBlock` function in `WrappingRule.kt` is being triggered for this block. The block is inside a raw string template. When `max_line_length` is exceeded (possibly due to the long table name), the rule incorrectly emits an error about a missing newline.

The fix should be to skip processing blocks that are inside a string template (raw string literal). We can use `isPartOfString()` to check this.

content.thinking:
Now I understand the issue! The `beforeVisitBlock` function is checking whether a block should be wrapped. The problem is at line 144-154, when `maxLineLength > 0`. It calculates the line length based on the leaves between the start and end of the line, and if the line exceeds `maxLineLength`, it sets `wrapBlock = true`.

However, the problem is that when the block is inside a raw string template, this check shouldn't apply at all. Raw strings can be as long as needed. The string template entry inside a raw string, like `${query.gameId?.let { "AND id = ?" } ?: ""}`, contains a lambda `{ "AND id = ?" }` with a BLOCK element. 

When the raw string literal contains a long line (like `table_name_long_enough_so_max_line_length_is_met_and_wrapping_rule_is_violated_and_generates_an_error`), the line length calculation incorrectly triggers the wrapping for the block inside the string template.

The fix is to skip blocks that are part of a string template. We should add a check at the beginning of `beforeVisitBlock` to return early if the block is part of a string.

Welcome to Gradle 7.6!

Here are the highlights of this release:
 - Added support for Java 19.
 - Introduced `--rerun` flag for individual task rerun.
 - Improved dependency block for test suites to be strongly typed.
 - Added a pluggable system for Java toolchains provisioning.

For more details see https://docs.gradle.org/7.6/release-notes.html

To honour the JVM settings for this build a single-use Daemon process will be forked. See https://docs.gradle.org/7.6/userguide/gradle_daemon.html#sec:disabling_the_daemon.
Daemon will be stopped at the end of the build 
Type-safe project accessors is an incubating feature.

content.thinking:
All lints are clean. Now let me summarize what was done:

## Summary

The issue was that the `wrapping` rule was incorrectly reporting a false positive "Missing newline after `{`" when using `let` blocks (or other lambda expressions) inside raw string literals.

### Root Cause

The `beforeVisitBlock` function in `WrappingRule.kt` was checking if a block should be wrapped based on:
1. Whether the block contains newlines
2. Whether the line length exceeds `max_line_length`

However, it wasn't considering that blocks inside string templates should be exempt from this rule. When a raw string literal contains a long line (exceeding `max_line_length`) and also contains a lambda expression like `${query.gameId?.let { "AND id = ?" } ?: ""}`, the line length calculation was incorrectly triggering the wrapping logic for the block inside the string template.

### Fix

1. Added the import for `isPartOfString` from `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast`
2. Added a check at the beginning of `beforeVisitBlock` to skip blocks that are part of a string template
3. Added the same check in `afterVisitChildNodes` for consistency

The check uses `node.treeParent.isPartOfString()` which verifies if the parent of the block is inside a STRING_TEMPLATE element.

### Tests

Created a new test file `WrappingRuleRawStringTest.kt` with tests covering:
1. A `let` block inside a raw string literal with a long line
2. A multiline `let` block inside a raw string literal
3. A simple block inside a raw string literal
4. A `let` block inside a raw string literal on a single line
5. A `run` block inside a raw string literal
6. A block inside a regular string template

All tests pass, including all existing tests in the `ktlint-ruleset-standard` module.

content.text:
All tests pass. Let me provide a summary of the changes I made:

## Summary of Changes

### Problem
The `wrapping` rule was incorrectly reporting a false positive "Missing newline after `{`" when using `let` blocks (or other lambda expressions) inside raw string literals, especially when the line length exceeded `max_line_length`.

### Root Cause
The `beforeVisitBlock` function in `WrappingRule.kt` was checking whether a block should be wrapped based on line length, but it wasn't considering that blocks inside string templates should be exempt from this wrapping logic. When a raw string literal contains a long line and also contains a lambda expression like `${query.gameId?.let { "AND id = ?" } ?: ""}`, the line length calculation was incorrectly triggering wrapping for the block inside the string template.

### Fix
1. **Added the import** for `isPartOfString` from `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast`

2. **Modified `beforeVisitBlock`** to skip blocks that are part of a string template by adding an early return:
   ```kotlin
   if (node.treeParent.isPartOfString()) {
       return
   }
   ```

3. **Modified `afterVisitChildNodes`** with the same check for consistency.

### Files Changed
- `/project/ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/WrappingRule.kt`

### New Test File Created
- `/project/ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/test/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/WrappingRuleRawStringTest.kt`

This test file contains 6 test cases covering various scenarios of blocks inside string templates:
- `let` blocks inside raw strings with long lines
- Multiline `let` blocks inside raw strings
- Simple blocks inside raw strings
- `let` blocks on a single line
- `run` blocks inside raw strings
- Blocks inside regular (non-raw) string templates

230â†’            // if (true &&
231â†’            //     true
232â†’            // ) {
233â†’            // }
234â†’            // instead of
235â†’            // if (
236â†’            //     true &&
237â†’            //     true
238â†’            // ) {
239â†’            // }
240â†’            node.treeNext?.elementType != CONDITION
241â†’        ) {
242â†’            requireNewlineAfterLeaf(node, autoCorrect, emit)
243â†’        }
244â†’        if (!r.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
245â†’            requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(r, autoCorrect, emit, node.treeParent.lineIndent())
246â†’        }
247â†’    }

249â†’    private fun rearrangeSuperTypeList(
250â†’        node: ASTNode,
251â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
252â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
253â†’    ) {
254â†’        val entries = (node.psi as KtSuperTypeList).entries
255â†’        if (
256â†’            node.textContains('\n') &&
257â†’            entries.size > 1 &&
258â†’            // e.g.
259â†’            //
260â†’            // class A : B, C,
261â†’            //     D
262â†’            // or
263â†’            // class A : B, C({
264â†’            // }), D
265â†’            //
266â†’            // but not
267â†’            //
268â†’            // class A : B, C, D({
269â†’            // })
270â†’            !(
271â†’                entries.dropLast(1).all { it.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY } &&
272â†’                    entries.last().elementType == SUPER_TYPE_CALL_ENTRY
273â†’                )
274â†’        ) {
275â†’            // put space after :
276â†’            if (!node.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
277â†’                val colon = node.prevCodeLeaf()!!
278â†’                if (
279â†’                    !colon.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
280â†’                    colon.prevCodeLeaf().let { it?.elementType != RPAR || !it.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
281â†’                ) {

283â†’                }
284â†’            }
285â†’            // put entries on separate lines
286â†’            // TODO: group emit()s below with the one above into one (similar to ParameterListWrappingRule)
287â†’            for (c in node.children()) {
288â†’                if (c.elementType == COMMA &&
289â†’                    !c.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
290â†’                    !c.isFollowedByCommentOnSameLine()
291â†’                ) {
292â†’                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
293â†’                        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired = c,
294â†’                        autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
295â†’                        emit = emit,

297â†’                    )
298â†’                }

305â†’            ?.isPartOfComment() == true

307â†’    private fun rearrangeValueList(
308â†’        node: ASTNode,
309â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
310â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
311â†’    ) {
312â†’        for (c in node.children()) {
313â†’            val hasLineBreak = when (c.elementType) {
314â†’                VALUE_ARGUMENT -> c.hasLineBreak(LAMBDA_EXPRESSION, FUN)
315â†’                VALUE_PARAMETER, ANNOTATION -> c.hasLineBreak()
316â†’                else -> false
317â†’            }
318â†’            if (hasLineBreak) {
319â†’                // rearrange
320â†’                //
321â†’                // a, b, value(
322â†’                // ), c, d
323â†’                //
324â†’                // to
325â†’                //
326â†’                // a, b,
327â†’                // value(
328â†’                // ),
329â†’                // c, d
330â†’
331â†’                // insert \n in front of multi-line value
332â†’                val prevSibling = c.prevSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
333â†’                if (
334â†’                    prevSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
335â†’                    !prevSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
336â†’                ) {
337â†’                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(prevSibling, autoCorrect, emit)
338â†’                }
339â†’                // insert \n after multi-line value
340â†’                val nextSibling = c.nextSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
341â†’                val hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter =
342â†’                    c.isLastValueParameter() && c.firstChildNode.elementType == DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION
343â†’                if (
344â†’                    nextSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
345â†’                    !hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter &&
346â†’                    !nextSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
347â†’                    // value(
348â†’                    // ), // a comment
349â†’                    // c, d
350â†’                    nextSibling.treeNext?.treeNext?.psi !is PsiComment
351â†’                ) {
352â†’                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(nextSibling, autoCorrect, emit)
353â†’                }
354â†’            }
355â†’        }
356â†’    }

362â†’    private fun rearrangeClosingQuote(
363â†’        node: ASTNode,
364â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
365â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
366â†’    ) {
367â†’        node
368â†’            .treeParent
369â†’            .takeIf { it.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE }
370â†’            ?.let { it.psi as KtStringTemplateExpression }
371â†’            ?.takeIf { it.isMultiLine() }
372â†’            ?.takeIf { it.isFollowedByTrimIndent() || it.isFollowedByTrimMargin() }
373â†’            ?.takeIf { node.treePrev.text.isNotBlank() }
374â†’            ?.let {
375â†’                // rewriting
376â†’                // """
377â†’                //     text
378â†’                // _""".trimIndent()
379â†’                // to
380â†’                // """
381â†’                //     text
382â†’                // _
383â†’                // """.trimIndent()
384â†’                emit(
385â†’                    node.startOffset,
386â†’                    "Missing newline before \"\"\"",
387â†’                    true,
388â†’                )
389â†’                if (autoCorrect) {
390â†’                    node as LeafPsiElement
391â†’                    node.rawInsertBeforeMe(LeafPsiElement(LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY, "\n"))
392â†’                }
393â†’                LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before (closing) \"\"\"" }
394â†’            }
395â†’    }

397â†’    private fun mustBeFollowedByNewline(node: ASTNode): Boolean {
398â†’        // find EOL token (last token before \n)
399â†’        // if token is in lTokenSet
400â†’        //     find matching rToken
401â†’        //     return true if there is no newline after the rToken
402â†’        // return false
403â†’        val nextCodeSibling = node.nextCodeSibling() // e.g. BINARY_EXPRESSION
404â†’        var lToken = nextCodeSibling?.nextLeaf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }?.prevCodeLeaf()
405â†’        if (lToken != null && lToken.elementType !in LTOKEN_SET) {
406â†’            // special cases:
407â†’            // x = y.f({ z ->
408â†’            // })
409â†’            // x = y.f(0, 1,
410â†’            // 2, 3)
411â†’            lToken = lToken.prevLeaf { it.elementType in LTOKEN_SET || it == node }
412â†’        }
413â†’        if (lToken != null && lToken.elementType in LTOKEN_SET) {
414â†’            val rElementType = MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP[lToken.elementType]
415â†’            val rToken = lToken.nextSibling { it.elementType == rElementType }
416â†’            return rToken?.treeParent == lToken.treeParent
417â†’        }
418â†’        if (nextCodeSibling?.textContains('\n') == false) {
419â†’            return true
420â†’        }
421â†’        return false
422â†’    }

424â†’    private fun rearrangeArrow(
425â†’        node: ASTNode,
426â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
427â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
428â†’    ) {
429â†’        val p = node.treeParent
430â†’        if (
431â†’            // check
432â†’            // `{ p -> ... }`
433â†’            // and
434â†’            // `when { m -> ... }`
435â†’            // only
436â†’            p.elementType.let { it != FUNCTION_LITERAL && it != WHEN_ENTRY } ||
437â†’            // ... and only if expression after -> spans multiple lines
438â†’            !p.textContains('\n') ||
439â†’            // permit
440â†’            // when {
441â†’            //     m -> 0 + d({
442â†’            //     })
443â†’            // }
444â†’            (p.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY && mustBeFollowedByNewline(node)) ||
445â†’            // permit
446â†’            // when (this) {
447â†’            //     in 0x1F600..0x1F64F, // Emoticons
448â†’            //     0x200D // Zero-width Joiner
449â†’            //     -> true
450â†’            // }
451â†’            (p.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY && node.prevLeaf()?.textContains('\n') == true)
452â†’        ) {
453â†’            return
454â†’        }
455â†’        if (!node.nextCodeLeaf()?.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
456â†’            requireNewlineAfterLeaf(node, autoCorrect, emit)
457â†’        }
458â†’        val r = node.nextSibling { it.elementType == RBRACE } ?: return
459â†’        if (!r.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
460â†’            requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(r, autoCorrect, emit, node.lineIndent())
461â†’        }
462â†’    }

464â†’    private fun requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
465â†’        node: ASTNode,
466â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
467â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
468â†’        indent: String,
469â†’    ) {
470â†’        emit(
471â†’            node.startOffset - 1,
472â†’            """Missing newline before "${node.text}"""",
473â†’            true,
474â†’        )
475â†’        LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + ((if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before ${node.text}") }
476â†’        if (autoCorrect) {
477â†’            node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe("\n" + indent)
478â†’        }
479â†’    }

481â†’    private fun requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
482â†’        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired: ASTNode,
483â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
484â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
485â†’        indent: String? = null,
486â†’        nodeToFix: ASTNode = nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired,
487â†’    ) {
488â†’        emit(
489â†’            nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.startOffset + 1,
490â†’            """Missing newline after "${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"""",
491â†’            true,
492â†’        )
493â†’        LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline after ${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}" }
494â†’        if (autoCorrect) {
495â†’            val tempIndent = indent ?: (nodeToFix.lineIndent() + indentConfig.indent)
496â†’            nodeToFix.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe("\n" + tempIndent)
497â†’        }
498â†’    }

1â†’# Changelog
  2â†’All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3â†’This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4â†’
  5â†’## Unreleased
  6â†’
  7â†’### Added
  8â†’
  9â†’### Removed
 10â†’
 11â†’### Fixed
 12â†’* Do not enable the experimental rules by default when `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` or `ktlint_disabled_rules` are set. ([#1771](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1771))
 13â†’* A function signature not having any parameters which exceeds the `max-line-length` should be ignored by rule `function-signature` ([#1773](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1773))
 14â†’
 15â†’### Changed
 16â†’
 17â†’## [0.48.1] - 2023-01-03
 18â†’
 19â†’### Added
 20â†’
 21â†’### Removed
 22â†’
 23â†’### Fixed
 24â†’
 25â†’* An enumeration class having a primary constructor and in which the list of enum entries is followed by a semicolon then do not remove the semicolon in case it is followed by code element `no-semi` ([#1733](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1733))
 26â†’* Add API so that KtLint API consumer is able to process a Kotlin script snippet without having to specify a file path ([#1738](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1738))
 27â†’* Disable the `standard:filename` rule whenever Ktlint CLI is run with option `--stdin` ([#1742](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1742))
 28â†’* Fix initialization of the logger when `--log-level` is specified. Throw exception when an invalid value is passed. ([#1749](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1749))
 29â†’* Fix loading of custom rule set JARs.
 30â†’* Rules provided via a custom rule set JAR (Ktlint CLI) or by an API provider are enabled by default. Only rules in the `experimental` rule set are disabled by default. ([#1747](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1747))
 31â†’
 32â†’### Changed
 33â†’
 34â†’* Update Kotlin development version to `1.8.0` and Kotlin version to `1.8.0`.
 35â†’
 36â†’## [0.48.0] - 2022-12-15
 37â†’
 38â†’### Indent rule
 39â†’
 40â†’The `indent` rule has been rewritten from scratch. Solving problems in the old algorithm was very difficult. With the new algorithm this becomes a lot easier. Although the new implementation of the rule has been compared against several open source projects containing over 400,000 lines of code, it is still likely that new issues will be discovered. Please report your indentation issues so that these can be fixed as well.
 41â†’
 42â†’### `.editorconfig` property to disable rules
 43â†’
 44â†’In the previous release (0.47.x), the `.editorconfig`  property `disabled_rules` was deprecated and replaced with `ktlint_disabled_rules`. This latter property has now been deprecated as well in favour of a more flexible and better maintainable solution. Rule and rule sets can now be enabled/disabled with a separate property per rule (set). Please read [deprecation of (ktlint_)disable_rules property](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/faq/#why-is-editorconfig-property-disabled_rules-deprecated-and-how-do-i-resolve-this) for more information.
 45â†’
 46â†’The KtLint CLI has not been changed. Although you can still use parameter `--experimental` to enable KtLint's Experimental rule set, you might want to set `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental = enabled` instead.
 47â†’
 48â†’### API Changes & RuleSet providers
 49â†’
 50â†’If you are not an API consumer or Rule Set provider then you can skip this section.
 51â†’
 52â†’#### Class relocations
 53â†’
 54â†’Classes below have been relocated:
 55â†’
 56â†’* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.UsesEditorConfigProperties.EditorConfigProperty` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfigProperty`. 
 57â†’* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.KtLintParseException` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.KtLintParseException`.
 58â†’* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleExecutionException` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.KtLintRuleException`.
 59â†’* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.format.internal.Color` has been moved to `com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.format.Color`.
 60â†’* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.plain.internal.Color` has been moved to `com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.plain.Color`.
 61â†’
 62â†’#### Invoking `lint` and `format`
 63â†’
 64â†’This is the last release that supports the `ExperimentalParams` to invoke the `lint` and `format` functions of KtLint. The `ExperimentalParams` contains a mix of configuration settings which are not dependent on the file/code which is to be processed. Other parameters in that class describe the code/file to be processed but can be configured inconsistently (for example a file with name "foo.kt" could be marked as a Kotlin Script file).
 65â†’
 66â†’The static object `KtLint` is deprecated and replaced by class `KtLintRuleEngine` which is configured with `KtLintRuleEngineConfiguration`. The instance of the `KtLintRuleEngine` is intended to be reused for scanning all files in a project and should not be recreated per file.
 67â†’
 68â†’Both `lint` and `format` are simplified and can now be called for a code block or for an entire file.
 69â†’
 70â†’```kotlin
 71â†’import java.io.File
 72â†’
 73â†’// Define a reusable instance of the KtLint Rule Engine
 74â†’val ktLintRuleEngine = KtLintRuleEngine(
 75â†’  // Define configuration
 76â†’)
 77â†’
 78â†’
 79â†’// Process a collection of files
 80â†’val files: Set<File> // Collect files in a convenient way
 81â†’files.forEach(file in files) {
 82â†’    ktLintRuleEngine.lint(file) {
 83â†’        // Handle lint violations
 84â†’    }
 85â†’}
 86â†’
 87â†’// or process a code sample for a given filepath
 88â†’ktLintRuleEngine.lint(
 89â†’  code = "code to be linted",
 90â†’  filePath = Path("/path/to/source/file")
 91â†’) {
 92â†’  // Handle lint violations
 93â†’}
 94â†’```
 95â†’
 96â†’#### Retrieve `.editorconfig`s
 97â†’
 98â†’The list of `.editorconfig` files which will be accessed by KtLint when linting or formatting a given path can now be retrieved with the new API `KtLint.editorConfigFilePaths(path: Path): List<Path>`. 
 99â†’
100â†’This API can be called with either a file or a directory. It's intended usage is that it is called once with the root directory of a project before actually linting or formatting files of that project. When called with a directory path, all `.editorconfig` files in the directory or any of its subdirectories (except hidden directories) are returned. In case the given directory does not contain an `.editorconfig` file or if it does not contain the `root=true` setting, the parent directories are scanned as well until a root `.editorconfig` file is found.
101â†’
102â†’Calling this API with a file path results in the `.editorconfig` files that will be accessed when processing that specific file. In case the directory in which the file resides does not contain an `.editorconfig` file or if it does not contain the `root=true` setting, the parent directories are scanned until a root `.editorconfig` file is found.
103â†’
104â†’#### Psi filename replaces FILE_PATH_USER_DATA_KEY
105â†’
106â†’Constant `KtLint.FILE_PATH_USER_DATA_KEY` is deprecated and will be removed in KtLint version 0.49.0. The file name will be passed correctly to the node with element type FILE and can be retrieved as follows:
107â†’```kotlin
108â†’if (node.isRoot()) {
109â†’    val fileName = (node.psi as? KtFile)?.name
110â†’    ...
111â†’}
112â†’```
113â†’
114â†’### Added
115â†’* Wrap blocks in case the max line length is exceeded or in case the block contains a new line `wrapping` ([#1643](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issue/1643))
116â†’* patterns can be read in from `stdin` with the `--patterns-from-stdin` command line options/flags ([#1606](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1606))
117â†’* Add basic formatting for context receiver in `indent` rule and new experimental rule `context-receiver-wrapping` ([#1672](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issue/1672))
118â†’* Add naming rules for classes and objects (`class-naming`), functions (`function-naming`) and properties (`property-naming`) ([#44](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issue/44))
119â†’* Add new built-in reporter `plain-summary` which prints a summary the number of violation which have been autocorrected or could not be autocorrected, both split by rule. 
120â†’
121â†’
122â†’### Fixed
123â†’
124â†’* Let a rule process all nodes even in case the rule is suppressed for a node so that the rule can update the internal state ([#1644](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issue/1644))
125â†’* Read `.editorconfig` when running CLI with options `--stdin` and `--editorconfig` ([#1651](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issue/1651))
126â†’* Do not add a trailing comma in case a multiline function call argument is found but no newline between the arguments `trailing-comma-on-call-site` ([#1642](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issue/1642))
127â†’* Add missing `ktlint_disabled_rules` to exposed `editorConfigProperties` ([#1671](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issue/1671))
128â†’* Do not add a second trailing comma, if the original trailing comma is followed by a KDOC `trailing-comma-on-declaration-site` and `trailing-comma-on-call-site` ([#1676](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issue/1676))
129â†’* A function signature preceded by an annotation array should be handled similar as function preceded by a singular annotation `function-signature` ([#1690](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issue/1690))
130â†’* Fix offset of annotation violations
131â†’* Fix line offset when blank line found between class and primary constructor
132â†’* Remove needless blank line between class followed by EOL, and primary constructor
133â†’* Fix offset of unexpected linebreak before assignment
134â†’* Remove whitespace before redundant semicolon if the semicolon is followed by whitespace 
135â†’
136â†’### Changed
137â†’* Update Kotlin development version to `1.8.0-RC` and Kotlin version to `1.7.21`.
138â†’* The default value for trailing comma's on call site is changed to `true` unless the `android codestyle` is enabled. Note that KtLint from a consistency viewpoint *enforces* the trailing comma on call site while default IntelliJ IDEA formatting only *allows* the trailing comma but leaves it up to the developer's discretion. ([#1670](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1670))
139â†’* The default value for trailing comma's on declaration site is changed to `true` unless the `android codestyle` is enabled. Note that KtLint from a consistency viewpoint *enforces* the trailing comma on declaration site while default IntelliJ IDEA formatting only *allows* the trailing comma but leaves it up to the developer's discretion. ([#1669](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1669))
140â†’* CLI options `--debug`, `--trace`, `--verbose` and `-v` are replaced with `--log-level=<level>` or the short version `-l=<level>, see [CLI log-level](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/install/cli/#logging). ([#1632](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issue/1632))
141â†’* In CLI, disable logging entirely by setting `--log-level=none` or `-l=none` ([#1652](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issue/1652))
142â†’* Rewrite `indent` rule. Solving problems in the old algorithm was very difficult. With the new algorithm this becomes a lot easier. Although the new implementation of the rule has been compared against several open source projects containing over 400,000 lines of code, it is still likely that new issues will be discovered. Please report your indentation issues so that these can be fixed as well. ([#1682](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1682), [#1321](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1321), [#1200](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1200), [#1562](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1562), [#1563](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1563), [#1639](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1639))
143â†’* Add methods "ASTNode.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe" and "ASTNode.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe" as replacements for "LeafElement.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe" and "LeafElement.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe". The new methods are more versatile and allow code to be written more readable in most places. ([#1687](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1687))
144â†’* Rewrite `indent` rule. Solving problems in the old algorithm was very difficult. With the new algorithm this becomes a lot easier. Although the new implementation of the rule has been compared against several open source projects containing over 400,000 lines of code, it is still likely that new issues will be discovered. Please report your indentation issues so that these can be fixed as well. ([#1682](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1682), [#1321](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1321), [#1200](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1200), [#1562](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1562), [#1563](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1563), [#1639](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1639), [#1688](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1688))
145â†’* Add support for running tests on `java 19`, remove support for running tests on `java 18`.
146â†’* Update `io.github.detekt.sarif4k:sarif4k` version to `0.2.0` ([#1701](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1701)).

148â†’## [0.47.1] - 2022-09-07
149â†’
150â†’### Fixed
151â†’* Do not add trailing comma in empty parameter/argument list with comments (`trailing-comma-on-call-site`, `trailing-comma-on-declaration-site`) ([#1602](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issue/1602))
152â†’* Fix class cast exception when specifying a non-string editorconfig setting in the default ".editorconfig" ([#1627](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issue/1627))
153â†’* Fix indentation before semi-colon when it is pushed down after inserting a trailing comma  ([#1609](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issue/1609))
154â†’* Do not show deprecation warning about property "disabled_rules" when using CLi-parameter `--disabled-rules` ([#1599](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1599))
155â†’* Traversing directory hierarchy at Windows ([#1600](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1600))
156â†’* Ant-style path pattern support ([#1601](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1601))
157â†’* Apply `@file:Suppress` on all toplevel declarations ([#1623](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1623))

162â†’## [0.47.0] - 2022-08-19
163â†’
164â†’### API Changes & RuleSet providers
165â†’
166â†’If you are not an API consumer nor a RuleSet provider, then you can safely skip this section. Otherwise, please read below carefully and upgrade your usage of ktlint. In this and coming releases, we are changing and adapting important parts of our API in order to increase maintainability and flexibility for future changes. Please avoid skipping a releases as that will make it harder to migrate.
167â†’
168â†’#### Rule lifecycle hooks / deprecate RunOnRootOnly visitor modifier
169â†’
170â†’Up until ktlint 0.46 the Rule class provided only one life cycle hook. This "visit" hook was called in a depth-first-approach on all nodes in the file. A rule like the IndentationRule used the RunOnRootOnly visitor modifier to call this lifecycle hook for the root node only in combination with an alternative way of traversing the ASTNodes. Downside of this approach was that suppression of the rule on blocks inside a file was not possible ([#631](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/631)). More generically, this applied to all rules, applying alternative traversals of the AST.
171â†’
172â†’The Rule class now offers new life cycle hooks:
173â†’* beforeFirstNode: This method is called once before the first node is visited. It can be used to initialize the state of the rule before processing of nodes starts. The ".editorconfig" properties (including overrides) are provided as parameter.
174â†’* beforeVisitChildNodes: This method is called on a node in AST before visiting its child nodes. This is repeated recursively for the child nodes resulting in a depth first traversal of the AST. This method is the equivalent of the "visit" life cycle hooks. However, note that in KtLint 0.48, the UserData of the rootnode no longer provides access to the ".editorconfig" properties. This method can be used to emit Lint Violations and to autocorrect if applicable.
175â†’* afterVisitChildNodes: This method is called on a node in AST after all its child nodes have been visited. This method can be used to emit Lint Violations and to autocorrect if applicable.
176â†’* afterLastNode: This method is called once after the last node in the AST is visited. It can be used for teardown of the state of the rule.
177â†’
178â†’Optionally, a rule can stop the traversal of the remainder of the AST whenever the goal of the rule has been achieved. See KDoc on Rule class for more information.
179â†’
180â†’The "visit" life cycle hook will be removed in Ktlint 0.48. In KtLint 0.47 the "visit" life cycle hook will be called *only* when hook "beforeVisitChildNodes" is not overridden. It is recommended to migrate to the new lifecycle hooks in KtLint 0.47. Please create an issue, in case you need additional assistence to implement the new life cycle hooks in your rules.
181â†’
182â†’
183â†’#### Ruleset providing by Custom Rule Set Provider
184â†’
185â†’The KtLint engine needs a more fine-grained control on the instantiation of new Rule instances. Currently, a new instance of a rule can be created only once per file. However, when formatting files the same rule instance is reused for a second processing iteration in case a Lint violation has been autocorrected. By re-using the same rule instance, state of that rule might leak from the first to the second processing iteration.
186â†’
187â†’Providers of custom rule sets have to migrate the custom rule set JAR file. The current RuleSetProvider interface which is implemented in the custom rule set is deprecated and marked for removal in KtLint 0.48. Custom rule sets using the old RuleSetProvider interface will not be run in KtLint 0.48 or above.
188â†’
189â†’For now, it is advised to implement the new RuleSetProviderV2 interface without removing the old RuleSetProvider interface. In this way, KtLint 0.47 and above use the RuleSetProviderV2 interface and ignore the old RuleSetProvider interface completely. KtLint 0.46 and below only use the old RuleSetProvider interface.
190â†’
191â†’Adding the new interface is straight forward, as can be seen below:
192â†’
193â†’```
194â†’// Current implementation
195â†’public class CustomRuleSetProvider : RuleSetProvider {
196â†’    override fun get(): RuleSet = RuleSet(
197â†’        "custom",
198â†’        CustomRule1(),
199â†’        CustomRule2(),
200â†’    )
